auth_notes: Requires OAuth2 (Client Credentials or Authorization Code). Crucially, stateful OData services require a 'fetch' request to obtain an x-csrf-token and session cookies before any POST/PATCH/DELETE operation can be performed.
rate_limits: Tenant-specific; typically defined in SAP API Management or Oracle Integration Cloud (OIC) throttling policies.
gotchas: CSRF token validation requires maintaining cookies across multiple HTTP Request nodes in the same execution.
OData protocol versioning (v2 vs v4) significantly changes the payload structure and query parameters.
Deep-insert operations (creating a header and lines in one call) have very specific schema requirements.
client_must_provide: Technical User with specific Scope/Role assignments
research_notes: Integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. A 'Pre-flight' HTTP node is required to GET the x-csrf-token with the header 'x-csrf-token: fetch'. The resulting token and 'set-cookie' headers must be passed into the subsequent 'Main' HTTP node. For SAP, the SAP API Business Hub provides the necessary OData specifications.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. A 'Pre-flight' HTTP node is required to GET the x-csrf-token with the header 'x-csrf-token: fetch'. The resulting token and 'set-cookie' headers must be passed into the subsequent 'Main' HTTP node. For SAP, the SAP API Business Hub provides the necessary OData specifications.
